title: The DI hierarchy and provider scope
description: Angular resolves a dependency up a tree of injectors, so where a service is provided determines its instance count and lifetime.
body: Use when wiring services or debugging shared/split state. Angular has a hierarchical injector: a request resolves from the element injector of the requesting component up through its ancestors to the root (and the module/environment injector). providedIn: 'root' yields a single app-wide instance; a component- or route-level provider creates a new instance scoped to that subtree's lifetime. Multiple providers for the same token at different levels create multiple instances. Do not assume a service is a singleton just because it has one class. Actions: choose the level that matches the intended sharing and lifetime. Validation signals include a test asserting the expected instance count. Risk boundary: an accidental extra instance silently splits state, so writes in one component are invisible to another.
